Welcome from the 
ACE Committee
Distinguished leaders from the Americas and beyond,
We are honored to welcome you to the Americas Competitiveness Exchange 
(ACE) Special Edition in Armenia 2024. ACE is a powerful mechanism that fosters 
cooperation and creation of new partnerships to strengthen economic development 
in the Americas and beyond.
ACE Armenia is convened by the Republic of Armenia through the Ministry of 
Economy with the support of the Government of the United States’ Department of 
Commerce and Department of State - Permanent Mission to the Organization of 
American States (OAS), and the assistance of the General Secretariat of the OAS, as 
Technical Secretariat of the Inter-American Competitiveness Network (RIAC).
Throughout the week of September 30- October 4, 2024, you will have the 
opportunity to explore and expand numerous collaborations with Armenia’s 
key stakeholders from the public, private and academic sectors to explore 
cooperation opportunities in investment, trade and business development, as 
well as spark research exchanges and build new partnerships to advance inclusive 
and sustainable economic development. 
The agenda for ACE Armenia offers opportunities to connect with the country’s 
top companies, universities, and innovation centers in the following clusters: 
Information and Communication Technology (ICT) and Engineering; Agriculture 
and Agribusiness; Sustainability and Renewable Energy; Talent Development and 
STEM Education; Tourism and the Cultural Economy.
We want to emphasize the importance of nurturing the connections you establish 
throughout the week to ensure successful outcomes. We are excited for what 
lies ahead of us and the collaboration and partnerships that will emerge from this 
week. To facilitate meaningful and strategic engagement throughout the program, 
we have compiled this Tripbook with key information about the ACE Armenia 
agenda and participants. We encourage you to use this resource as a compass to 
identify potential collaborations and business partners for trade deals, research and 
development, new ventures, soft-landing arrangements, technical exchanges, and 
any other forms of collaboration.
We wish to express our most sincere gratitude to the Republic of Armenia, our host, 
for the exceptional agenda they have prepared in collaboration with other national 
stakeholders.
We look forward to our work together.
Sincerely,
The ACE Committee

ARMENIA
Overview
What Sets Armenia Apart
Armenia is known for its 
talented workforce, 
particularly in industries 
such as technology 
and engineering. 
The country places 
a strong emphasis 
on education, with a 
well-developed system 
that includes universities and 
vocational schools.
Positioned at the crossroads of 
Europe and Asia, Armenia 
serves as a gateway 
to multiple markets. 
Its proximity to the 
European Union, the 
Middle East, and the 
Commonwealth of 
Independent States 
(CIS) offers opportunities 
for trade and access to a diverse 
customer base.
Armenia offers access to various 
funding sources and support 
mechanisms for startups 
and businesses. Business 
incubators, accelerators, 
and venture capital 
funds provide resources, 
mentorship, and 
financial backing to foster 
entrepreneurial growth.
Armenia is emerging as a 
technology hub, with a 
vibrant startup ecosystem 
and a rapidly growing 
ICT sector. The country’s 
focus on information 
technology, software 
development, and 
innovation has attracted 
global attention and investment. 
The Armenian government 
has implemented various 
reforms to enhance the 
ease of doing business, 
streamline regulations, 
and attract foreign 
investment. Initiatives 
such as tax incentives, 
simplified procedures, 
and business-friendly 
policies have contributed to a 
favorable business climate.
Armenia’s rich cultural heritage, 
historical sites, and natural 
beauty make it an 
attractive destination for 
tourism. The country’s 
efforts to promote 
its tourism industry 
offer opportunities for 
businesses in hospitality, 
travel, and related sectors.

ACE Site
Visits
SEPTEMBER 30 - OCTOBER 4, 2024
YEREVAN
7

8
American University of Armenia 
The 
American 
University 
of 
Armenia 
(AUA) 
is 
a 
distinguished private institution of higher education in 
Armenia, offering a world-class academic experience that 
combines international standards with a local context. 
Affiliated with the University of California and accredited 
by the WASC Senior College and University Commission, 
AUA is committed to providing a high-quality, graduate, 
and 
undergraduate 
education 
that 
fosters 
civic 
engagement and upholds democratic values. AUA fosters 
scholarship in a setting that values and develops academic 
excellence, free inquiry, integrity, scholarship, leadership, 
and service to society. AUA is currently the only university 
in the former Soviet Union that received WASC Senior 
College and University Commission accreditation. This is 
the testament that AUA consistently upholds the highest 
international standards in higher education, joining the 
ranks of top-rated institutions like Stanford University, 
the University of California Berkeley, and the University of 
Southern California, among others.
Matena is an Armenia-based international school that 
contributes to the development of professional skills 
and leadership qualities through collecting and sharing 
the best knowledge and the leading world practices 
for those who permanently pursue improvement and 
excellence in their professional fields. In cooperation 
with world-renowned partners and through combining 
the best local expertise with the high-level international 
experience, Matena seeks to become one of the leading 
centers of professional education both for Armenia and 
for the region.
Matena International School
TUMO Center for Creative Technologies
TUMO’s mission is to create life-changing learning 
experiences on a large scale. TUMO is an innovative, after 
school program, offering free of charge education to 
teens between the ages of 12 and 18 . Serving 30,000 
teens on a weekly basis across 13 locations worldwide, 
TUMO’s network includes centers in Armenia, France, 
Germany, Portugal, Switzerland, Ukraine and Albania 
with many more locations planned. TUMO centers 
provide teens free access to critical digital skills in 
technology and design – offering an on-ramp to highly 
skilled, fulfilling careers. TUMO combines mentorship 
and instruction from industry professionals with a 
cutting edge, proprietary software which delivers hyper-
personalized learning experiences to teens.

9
Engineering City
The Engineering City is an environment created for 
engineering companies in the high-tech sector with the 
purpose of facilitating and accelerating the development 
of complex engineering solutions. It is a platform where 
engineering, science, and education come together 
to tackle the advanced engineering challenges. It 
provides a full range of advanced equipment, research 
and prototyping labs, machine tooling and production 
facilities that are accessible to all the resident companies. 
We define an Engineering Company in the High-Tech 
sector as a company that uses measurement equipment, 
prototyping labs and machine tooling during the 
development and production of their products and 
solutions. The Engineering City Project is a Public-Private 
Partnership between the Government of Armenia and a 
Consortium of Private Companies.
Picsart is an Armenian-American technology company 
with its headquarters located in Miami, Florida and 
Yerevan, Armenia. The company specializes in the 
development of the Picsart suite, a collection of online 
photo and video editing applications combined with 
a social creative community. Picsart has achieved 
significant worldwide popularity and is recognized as 
one of the most widely used apps in its category. It has 
garnered over 1 billion downloads across 180 countries, 
demonstrating its extensive user base and global 
presence. In 2022, Picsart achieved unicorn status, a 
term used to describe a startup valued at over $1 billion, 
after raising $130 million in funding from SoftBank. This 
substantial investment underscores the recognition of 
Picsart’s success and potential in the market.Overall, 
Picsart has emerged as a prominent player in the online 
photo and video editing industry, offering users powerful 
editing tools while fostering a vibrant social creative 
community. Its remarkable growth and achievements 
have solidified its position as a leader in the field.
Picsart Headquarter
ServiceTitan
ServiceTitan is one of the Armenian-founded tech 
unicorns. CEO Ara Mahdessian and President Vahe 
Kuzoyan, both of Armenian descent, joined forces 
after college and founded ServiceTitan with a simple 
but challenging goal: to streamline their fathers’ home 
service businesses. Now, their company is responsible 
for transforming the entire home services industry. 
ServiceTitan is the world’s leading and fastest-growing 
software technology platform for the trades, a trillion-
dollar global industry. The company helps small 
business entrepreneurs run and grow their businesses, 
streamline operations, improve customer service, and 
increase sales. They currently have more than 12,000 
customers and more than 3500 employees across 
Glendale, Atlanta, and Yerevan.

10
Ardy Academy
Ardy Academy is a groundbreaking educational 
initiative focused on training individuals and teams 
for key digital professions that are integral to the 
fourth industrial revolution in the Republic of Armenia. 
Through an intensive training program, the academy 
aims to equip students with the skills they need for the 
digital age. This institution stands as the largest center 
for digital professions in Armenia.
Founded by Renderforest, Woods Center, is a multi-
purpose hub that offers a unique environment for 
business, education, events, and living. More than just a 
business center, Woods is a thoughtfully designed space 
where individuals can work, learn, and entertain, all within 
an inspiring and vibrant atmosphere. Delegates will 
explore how this innovative space fosters collaboration 
and creativity across various fields.
Woods Center
Yeremyan Tashir Farm
The Yeremyan Farm livestock complex, with an area of 
more than 30 hectares, includes cattle and pig farms, a 
slaughterhouse, a feed warehouse, fields, an automated 
milking parlor, parking for agricultural equipment, as well 
as the necessary infrastructure. The livestock complex 
comprises a cattle-breeding farm, featuring 2 buildings 
of livestock barns for 1500 animals providing 40-45 liters 
of milk per day, a rotary automated milking parlor, feed 
crushing machine and feed warehouse, a parking area 
for agricultural machinery, a spacious hay warehouse, 
silage, and manure disposal wells. The pig farm of the 
livestock complex, intended for 560-580 sows which will 
annually produce 17000 animals, has 3 livestock barns, a 
feed crushing machine and feed warehouse, and manure 
disposal wells. The goal of the project is to ensure food 
security, agriculture, and economic development of the 
Republic of Armenia.

11
Hin Areni Factory
Vayots Dzor and Areni village in particular has been the 
cradle of Armenian wine-making for millennia. “Hin Areni” 
wine factory opens up the doors of this region to the world 
to showcase the historic and indigenous Armenian grape 
varietals. At the winery the historic traditions of wine-
making are well combined with state-of-the-art modern 
equipment. “Hin Areni” wine factory has the capacity to 
process over 250 tonnes of grapes. 
What initially started as a modest project in 2007 evolved 
into one of the most remarkable archaeological discoveries 
in the realm of historic winemaking and the traditions of 
Neolithic civilizations. Nestled near the historic Areni village 
and the Arpa River, the Areni-1 Cave holds not only artifacts 
but also grape stems and seeds discovered at the base of 
amphorae, known as karas. These artifacts were carefully 
examined and the results unveiled a stunning revelation: 
the grape seeds date back an astonishing 6,200 years! This 
groundbreaking discovery confirms that the Areni-1 Cave is 
home to the earliest known winery in the world.
Children of Armenia Fund (COAF)
Children of Armenia Fund (COAF) is a non-profit 
organization 
dedicated 
to 
enhancing 
educational 
opportunities and infrastructure in rural areas of Armenia. 
COAF’s focus on delivering high-impact, sustainable 
initiatives encompasses formal and non-formal education, 
teacher training, and accessible medical care. 
One of COAF’s flagship initiatives, the COAF SMART 
program, represents an innovative approach to community 
and individual growth, aimed at improving the quality of 
life in rural communities. As part of this program, COAF 
has established a SMART center in Debed village, located 
in the Lori region of Armenia, fostering educational 
advancements that benefit individuals, society, and the 
environment.
COAF’s dedication extends to the hospitality sector, with 
the establishment of Concept Hotel by COAF in Debed. 
This social enterprise serves as a self-sustaining hospitality 
training initiative, with all proceeds directed towards 
charitable contributions to support COAF’s initiatives and 
projects.
NOA’s vineyards
NOA was founded to blend Armenia’s ancient 
winemaking traditions with modern practices. In 
partnership with one of the oldest wine houses in 
Switzerland, NOA focuses on cultivating indigenous 
grape varieties in the Vayots Dzor region. Committed 
to organic farming, NOA produces wines that reflect 
the rich heritage and promising future of Armenian 
viticulture.

12
The Gyumri Technology Center (GTC) has been established 
by the Enterprise Incubator Foundation, the RA Government 
and the World Bank. The goals of the Center include 
development of technical and business skills, promotion 
of technological entrepreneurship, commercialization of 
innovative research undertakings, creation of new technology 
companies, attracting of foreign investment and others.
 
Gyumri Information Technologies Center (GITC) stands 
as a beacon of technological innovation and economic 
empowerment in Armenia. Their mission is to transform lives 
through advanced education and strategic support, driving 
sustainable development in underserved communities. 
At GITC, they specialize in bridging the digital divide by 
offering cutting-edge training in IT, engineering, and 
entrepreneurship. Hallmark initiatives include the ‘Women 
Economic Empowerment Accelerator’, which uniquely 
supports single mothers and displaced women from Artsakh, 
fostering their transition from adversity to entrepreneurship 
through bespoke technology and business education. 
AI9 is an international startup campus located in Yerevan, 
Armenia, set to open by the end of 2024. With a strong 
focus on artificial intelligence, innovation, and fostering 
a vibrant community of visionary tech founders, AI9 is 
designed to be a dynamic hub for startups. The campus 
aims to strengthen the local tech ecosystem while 
providing the tools, resources, and global connections 
needed to help startups grow and scale internationally. 
By nurturing collaboration and innovation, AI9 will play a 
pivotal role in shaping the future of Armenia’s technology 
landscape.
AI9
SolarOn started its solar panel production activities on 
June 29, 2016, becoming the first Armenian manufacturer 
of solar panels. The brand ‘’SolarOn’’ is a registered 
trademark for products manufactured by Profpanel. Over 
the course of 8 years, Solaron has successfully launched 
and completed more than 1400 projects both in Armenia 
and abroad. Solaron’s services are available throughout all 
regions of Armenia.
SolarOn 
Gyumri Information 
Technologies Center (GITC)
